description:
Phase 5 of fz-ul4.23. Make the interpreter a real third path. Subsumes fz-ul4.19.8.

## Today

src/ir_interp.rs walks fz_ir::Module but uses value::Value (not FzValue), has no heap, and explicitly errors on spawn/receive. Declared in main.rs but no production consumer.

## Target

- Same input as JIT: fz_ir::Module.
- Same value rep: FzValue from fz_value.rs.
- Same heap: heap.rs allocators, per-Process via CURRENT_PROCESS TLS.
- Same runtime FFI: spawn/send/receive call into runtime.rs the way the JIT does.
- Wire up to the fixture matrix as the `interp` path. Concurrency fixtures must pass through interp.
- Migrate REPL and test_runner off the AST evaluator (eval.rs) onto the real interp for runtime execution. AST evaluator stays for macro expansion only.

## Acceptance

- `# paths: interp, jit` works for every fixture currently jit-only that doesn't use a deferred feature.
- concurrency_ping_pong.fz runs through interp without errors.
- REPL and `fz test` use the IR interp for execution.
- fz-ul4.19.8 closes.
title: Rebuild ir_interp on FzValue/heap/runtime substrate
